notes: Cross-reference of all raw Wave 1 sources for routes using Aurora Boulevard or the Aurora/Katipunan/Marcos Highway corridor. See analysis/validated-aurora-boulevard-corridor.md for full analysis.

routes.origin: Robinsons Antipolo, Antipolo City
routes.notes: Explicitly 'Via Aurora Boulevard' per ltoportal (MM-BUS-003) and wikimili (MM-BUS-003). Primary city bus spine for Aurora Blvd corridor. Two variants documented: (A) via Aurora Blvd, (B) via Cogeo/alternate Sumulong Hwy.

routes.origin: Welcome Rotonda, QC/Manila boundary
routes.destination: Aurora Blvd / Katipunan Ave. (LRT-2 Katipunan/Anonas area)
routes.frequency_notes: Mon-Fri peak: 20 min; off-peak: 30 min. Sat/Sun: 1 hr. Expanded hours June 2024.
routes.notes: QC Government electric bus. Free fare. Terminates on Aurora Blvd at LRT-2 Katipunan/Anonas area. Full stop list available in ltoportal-ph-routes.json.

routes.origin: QC Hall NHA Interchange, Diliman
routes.frequency_notes: Mon-Fri peak: 15 min; off-peak: 30 min. Sat/Sun: 1 hr.
routes.notes: QC Government electric bus. Free fare. Terminates at Aurora Blvd/Gilmore (LRT-2 Gilmore station). Full stop list available.

routes.route_name: QCityBus Route 7 — QC Hall–C5/Ortigas via Katipunan/Aurora
routes.origin: QC Hall NHA Interchange, Diliman
routes.frequency_notes: Mon-Fri peak: 15 min; off-peak: 30 min. Sat/Sun: 1 hr.
routes.notes: QC Government electric bus. Passes through Aurora/Katipunan (LRT-2) then continues south on Katipunan to C5. Full stop list available.

routes.origin: Montalban (Rodriguez), Rizal
routes.notes: Operator: Marikina Auto Line Transport Corporation (MALTC). Confirmed in city-bus-operators-routes.json. Uses Aurora Blvd corridor (Marikina → Cubao) before joining EDSA southbound.

routes.route_name: SSS Village, Marikina–Cubao via Aurora Blvd
routes.origin: Panorama Jeepney Terminal, SSS Village, Marikina City
routes.destination: Aurora Blvd / General Aguinaldo Ave. Intersection, Quezon City (Stop & Shop area)
key_stops: Asian College of Science and Technology (Aurora/JP Rizal)
routes.notes: LTFRB codes: T214 (older), T287 (current). Sakay ID: DOTR:R_SAKAY_PUJ_921. Full Aurora Blvd corridor from Marikina to Cubao. Stop-level data confirmed via Sakay Route Explorer.

routes.origin: Jeepney Terminal, Fortune Tobacco Corporation, Marikina City
routes.destination: Aurora Blvd / General Aguinaldo Ave. Intersection, Quezon City
routes.notes: LTFRB code T286. Sakay ID: DOTR:R_SAKAY_2018_PUJ_652. Traditional PUJ (not modern). Uses Aurora Blvd westbound to Cubao terminus.

routes.origin: Jeepney Terminal, Fortune Tobacco Corporation, Marikina City
routes.destination: Araneta City Modern Jeepney Terminal, Cubao
routes.notes: Modern PUJ (MPUJ). LTFRB code: MODERN-018. Sakay ID: DOTR:R_SAKAY_MPUJ_1143. Confirmed operational; modern vehicle type terminates at Araneta City terminal, not Stop & Shop.

routes.route_name: Calumpang, Marikina–LRT-2 Katipunan via Aurora Blvd
routes.origin: Calumpang, Marikina City
routes.notes: LTFRB code T242. Confirmed in lrt2-feeder-routes.json. Serves Santolan and Gilmore stations en route. LRT-2 feeder from Marikina Calumpang to Katipunan via Aurora Blvd.

routes.route_name: Calumpang, Marikina–Stop & Shop via Aurora Blvd
routes.origin: Calumpang, Marikina City
routes.notes: Sakay ID: LRT2-FEED-SAN-002. Full Aurora corridor from Calumpang to Cubao Stop & Shop terminus. Companion to T242 with extended western terminus.

routes.route_name: Cubao–Divisoria via Aurora Blvd / Sta. Mesa
routes.origin: Aurora Blvd, below LRT-2 Araneta Center-Cubao
routes.notes: Confirmed in cubao-terminal-routes.json as 'traditional jeepney; along Aurora Blvd.' Departs from Aurora Blvd below LRT-2 Cubao and heads westward toward Divisoria.

routes.route_name: Cubao–Quiapo via Aurora Blvd / Sta. Mesa
routes.origin: Aurora Blvd, below LRT-2 Araneta Center-Cubao
routes.notes: Confirmed in cubao-terminal-routes.json. Departs westward from Aurora Blvd. Also referenced as route code 227 in some community sources (Cubao-Quiapo via Sta. Mesa, C. Palanca).

routes.origin: Aurora/Lauan, Quezon City
routes.notes: LTFRB T-code T240. Confirmed in ltfrb-jeepney-routes.json. Western Aurora Blvd segment; connects Aurora to EDSA North Ave. (Quezon City north area).

routes.origin: General Romulo Ave. / Aurora Blvd. Intersection, Quezon City (Diamond terminal)
routes.notes: Sakay ID: DOTR:R_SAKAY_MPUJ_827. Modern PUJ. Originates at Aurora Blvd./Gen. Romulo intersection (the 'Diamond' terminal area in Cubao). Heads northwest toward Roces/Quezon City west. Confirms Aurora/Gen. Romulo as a distinct terminal node.

routes.route_name: Gen. B.G. Molina St., Marikina–Aurora Blvd / Gen. Aguinaldo Ave.
routes.origin: Gen. B.G. Molina St., Marikina City
routes.destination: Aurora Blvd / General Aguinaldo Ave. Intersection, Quezon City
routes.notes: Moovit line 7637727. 34 confirmed stops; longest Aurora Blvd jeepney documented. Operates until midnight (later than most routes). Source: moovitapp.com.

routes.origin: Araneta Center Jeepney Terminal (Telus side), Cubao
routes.destination: Manila East Rd. / Quezon, Angono, Rizal
key_stops: Asian College of Science and Technology (Aurora)
routes.notes: Sakay ID: DOTR:R_SAKAY_PUJ_1183. Uses Aurora Blvd for the initial Cubao segment before diverging toward Angono. Aurora Blvd segment confirmed via Sakay stop data.

routes.origin: Araneta Center Jeepney Terminal (Telus side), Cubao
routes.destination: Durian St., Silangan, San Mateo, Rizal
key_stops: Asian College of Science and Technology (Aurora)
routes.notes: Sakay ID: DOTR:R_SAKAY_PUJ_1162. Uses Aurora Blvd in the Cubao segment (confirmed stops at Aurora/Katipunan and nearby). Diverges at Marcos Highway toward San Mateo.

routes.origin: Araneta City Jeepney Terminal or P. Tuazon terminal, Cubao
routes.notes: Confirmed in cubao-terminal-routes.json. Uses Aurora Blvd heading east toward Cainta (in Rizal, just east of Marikina).

routes.origin: Araneta City Jeepney Terminal or P. Tuazon terminal, Cubao
routes.notes: Confirmed in cubao-terminal-routes.json. Continues Aurora Blvd corridor beyond Marikina to Taytay, Rizal.

routes.origin: UV Express Terminal below LRT-2 Cubao / Farmers Market side
routes.notes: Confirmed in cubao-terminal-routes.json. UV Express; departs from Farmers Market side of Cubao. Fare ₱35–50. Faster than jeepney for Cubao–Marikina trip.

routes.origin: Brgy. Fortune, Marikina City
routes.destination: Cubao, Quezon City (Farmers Market UV Terminal)
routes.notes: Flat fare ₱21. Confirmed multiple web sources (aranetacity.com, thepoortraveler.net). One of the cheapest UV Express fares in the corridor.

routes.route_name: Cubao–Padilla via Ermin Garcia / Marcos Highway (UV Express)
routes.origin: Farmers Plaza terminal, Cubao
routes.notes: Fare ₱35. Junction near Aurora/Katipunan before diverging to Marcos Highway. Also uses Farmers Plaza terminal (different from Farmers Market UV terminal). Source: thepoortraveler.net.

routes.route_name: Antipolo (SM Masinag)–Makati (One Ayala / Ayala Malls Feliz)
routes.origin: SM Masinag, Antipolo City
routes.destination: One Ayala / Ayala Malls Feliz, Makati
routes.notes: Operator: RRCG Transport. Regular fare ₱140; discounted ₱112. Beep Card and Cash accepted. Special drop-offs at SM Megamall and Robinsons Galleria confirm Ortigas/C5 routing, not Aurora Blvd proper. Start at Marcos Highway terminus adjacent to Aurora Blvd corridor.
